target: data/sections.json — SEC-2b.1 stratification paragraph (replaces fallback caveat with Option-2 template-variable wiring per ISS-934)
kernel_of_truth: The original prose was scientifically correct at the moment it was written: of the 41 CA_NONE WINs (counted inclusively), exactly 7/15/12/3/2/2 fell into the listed verdict buckets. The author was not making numbers up. The problem is purely architectural — counts that move with the data set should never be hand-typed in prose, especially in a review that criticises the dome model for hardcoding values it claims to derive.
finding_audit_summary: Verified all six hardcoded count words against current data/wins.json (April 2026 build). Five of the six are still correct under the inclusive interpretation (all 70 wins). One is incorrect under the canonical {{CA_NONE}} interpretation: "Two are not demonstrated" should now read 1, because {{CA_NONE}} is computed from base WINs only (3-digit IDs, per the ISS-692 fix in generate-html.js line 977-978) and WIN-058b — the second Not-Demonstrated CA_NONE entry — is a sub-ID tracking entry. So the prose silently disagrees with its own {{CA_NONE}} = 40 by saying 7+15+12+3+2+2 = 41. The fallback caveat hides this drift but does not fix it; template variables eliminate it permanently.

current_counts_at_build.sub_id_excluded: 058b (Not Demonstrated, sub-ID tracking entry — counted inclusively but not in baseWins)

required_infrastructure_patch.patch_kind: two edits — extend codeAnalysis aggregation, then expose new template variables
required_infrastructure_patch.edit_1_location: inside the codeAnalysis IIFE (around line 973-991), before the closing return}
required_infrastructure_patch.edit_2_location: inside the substitutions map (around line 79, immediately after the {{CA_NONE}} entry)
required_infrastructure_patch.test_assertion: After patch, build emits prose containing "3 are refuted by data ... 15 misleading ... 12 standard-physics relabellings ... 2 unfalsifiable ... 7 self-contradicted ... 1 not demonstrated". Total = {{CA_NONE}} = 40.

prose_replacement.target_paragraph: data/sections.json -> part2b.html, the paragraph beginning "Stratifying the unmonitored WINs:"
prose_replacement.find_text: Stratifying the unmonitored WINs: (Counts from wins.json at build time.) The {{CA_NONE}} WINs with no monitoring code are not all equivalent failures. Two are theological assertions with no measurable content (WIN-032, WIN-034) — monitoring is not applicable. Seven are self-contradicted by the dome’s own geometry (WIN-044/050/051 tidal, WIN-053 flux, WIN-056 solar, WIN-061 P-wave, WIN-067 gravity) — monitoring would expose the contradiction. Fifteen are misleading (duplicated, circular, or non-discriminating). Twelve are standard-physics relabellings — monitoring would confirm the phenomenon but not the dome’s causal explanation. Three are refuted by published data — monitoring would expose the failure. Two are not demonstrated. The gaps are systematic: the dome’s monitoring avoids exactly the categories where testing would be most damaging.
prose_replacement.replace_text: Stratifying the unmonitored WINs: The {{CA_NONE}} WINs with no monitoring code are not all equivalent failures. The breakdown by verdict at this build: {{CA_NONE_UNFAL}} are theological assertions with no measurable content (e.g. WIN-032, WIN-034) — monitoring is not applicable. {{CA_NONE_SELFCON}} are self-contradicted by the dome’s own geometry (e.g. WIN-044/050/051 tidal, WIN-053 flux, WIN-056 solar, WIN-061 P-wave, WIN-067 gravity) — monitoring would expose the contradiction. {{CA_NONE_MISLEADING}} are misleading (duplicated, circular, or non-discriminating). {{CA_NONE_STD}} are standard-physics relabellings — monitoring would confirm the phenomenon but not the dome’s causal explanation. {{CA_NONE_REFUTED}} are refuted by published data — monitoring would expose the failure. {{CA_NONE_NOTDEMO}} are not demonstrated. The gaps are systematic: the dome’s monitoring avoids exactly the categories where testing would be most damaging.
notes: Removed the "(Counts from wins.json at build time.)" caveat — once template variables are wired, the caveat is no longer needed because nothing in the paragraph is hardcoded.

Changed parenthetical lists from "(WIN-032, WIN-034)" to "(e.g. WIN-032, WIN-034)" so they remain accurate as the population grows. The exemplars stay illustrative; if a future Unfalsifiable CA_NONE WIN is added the prose still reads correctly.

Number-vs-word style: switched from English number words (Seven, Fifteen, ...) to digit substitutions ({{CA_NONE_SELFCON}} etc). This matches how {{CA_NONE}} already renders elsewhere in the paragraph ("The 40 WINs") and the rest of the document, which freely mixes digits with prose ("14 self-contradicted WINs (Part 2)" two paragraphs later).

holes_addressed: Hardcoded English-word counts (Seven, Fifteen, Twelve, Three, Two) decouple from the data and silently rot when verdicts change.

Existing prose silently disagrees with {{CA_NONE}}: hardcoded sum is 41 but {{CA_NONE}} = 40 because WIN-058b (sub-ID) is excluded from base counts. The fallback caveat hid this; template variables eliminate it.
Specific WIN-ID parentheticals (WIN-032, WIN-034) lock the prose to a snapshot of which WINs hold each verdict. Switched to "e.g. ..." exemplars so the prose stays defensible as the population grows.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not also turn the WIN-ID lists (WIN-044/050/051, etc.) into template variables?
anticipated_objections.response: Possible but high-risk and low-value. Template variables that emit lists need format-string handling (commas, slashes, conjunctions) that we do not have wiring for. The "e.g." exemplars are a 99% solution at zero infrastructure cost; if a future review wants full dynamic IDs, that is a separate (much larger) infrastructure expansion.

anticipated_objections.objection: Singular/plural grammar breaks if a count goes to 1 or 0 ("1 are refuted" reads badly).
anticipated_objections.response: Real concern. The "Not Demonstrated" bucket is currently 1 and the prose now reads "{{CA_NONE_NOTDEMO}} are not demonstrated" → "1 are not demonstrated". Acceptable trade vs hand-typed staleness; pluralisation helpers can be added later if any single bucket sits at 1 long enough to be embarrassing. Alternative phrasings ("Not demonstrated: {{CA_NONE_NOTDEMO}}.") were considered but reduce parallelism with the rest of the list.

anticipated_objections.objection: The fallback caveat is already in place — why bother rewiring?
anticipated_objections.response: The caveat is the dome model’s own pattern: a disclaimer instead of a fix. Our review explicitly criticises that pattern (e.g. WIN-002 hardcoded comparison, dome’s 94.5% accuracy claim). Leaving a caveat in our own prose where a 30-line patch eliminates the staleness entirely is exactly the kind of inconsistency the curmudgeon flags.

decider_action_required: 1. Apply the two-edit infrastructure patch to build-scripts/generate-html.js (additive — no existing wiring removed).
2. Apply the prose find/replace in data/sections.json -> part2b.html.
3. Rebuild HTML and verify the rendered paragraph contains digits 3 / 15 / 12 / 2 / 7 / 1 (summing to {{CA_NONE}} = 40 at this build).
4. After verification, mark EXP-158 status: complete, integrated_at: <timestamp>, integration_mode: "template-variable-wiring" (replacing the prior fallback-caveat mode).
notes_for_self: Wrote this as a follow-up to the decider’s 2026-04-14 fallback integration. The fallback (Option 3 — caveat) is currently shipped; this expansion provides Option 2 (template variables, the preferred approach per ISS-934 notes) so the decider can swap it in.
